releaseApp.notarization: not run; Apple credentials are not configured on this machine

coldLaunch.evidence: Computer Use quit the previously running exact release app, confirmed the process absent, cold-launched the exact current-worktree path, entered Dramaturgy Smoke Lab, selected 3D and maximized the stage.

ambientScenario.name: unaccelerated two-minute ambient observation
ambientScenario.method: The exact release CGWindow was recorded for 125.008 seconds without speed-up or concurrent GUI actions. Frames at 5, 30, 60, 90 and 120 seconds were extracted and visually inspected; all five were complete, non-black WebGL frames.
observed: At 60 seconds Marcus was visibly away from his assigned workstation in the central area.
At 90 seconds Maya was at the library while another ambient actor occupied the right-side area; away concurrency remained within the hard cap.
By 120 seconds the observed actors had returned, proving a complete outbound-dwell-return lifecycle rather than static placement.

microMovementEvidence.method: The existing 125.133-second release recording was decoded at 00:00, 00:02, 00:04, 00:06, 00:08, 00:10, 00:12, 00:14, 00:16 and 00:18 with a tight crop around the red-shirt seated Product actor.
microMovementEvidence.observed: Across those frames the actor's head, torso and hands visibly shift while the chair and workstation anchor remain fixed, proving the recording itself contains seated idle micro-motion in addition to the later away behavior.

focusQuietScenario.evidence: After selecting Focus in the release View options, an already-active ambient actor completed its return; the 25-second and 52-second samples then retained the same employee zone/workstation positions with no new away behavior.

runPreemptionScenario.interaction: A real direct-message Pi run was triggered with the user-authorized openrouter/free model. The conversation showed Typing and Stop, the Office view showed Marcus at his workstation under the real working performance, and the run completed with OK.
runPreemptionScenario.timingEvidence: The exact <=1 second interrupt is covered by the executable 66/66 ambient oracle; live Computer Use confirms the same real Pi event reaches the release scene and owns Marcus's visible performance.

piRuntime.configuration: The stale per-conversation glm-5.2 override first produced the expected missing-zai-key failure. Computer Use changed only this local conversation override to openrouter/free, retried, and received OK. No credential is stored in repository evidence and no Pi/provider source code changed.
